---
title: "Independent T-Test in R"
author: "Dr Lindokuhle Dlamini"
---

## Download Dataset

[Download the dataset used in this lesson](data/Independent_T_Test_Data.xlsx)

---

# Independent T-Test in R

The **independent t-test** compares the mean of two independent groups.

Example: comparing **plant species richness** between invaded and uninvaded sites.

---

# 1. Load Required Packages

```r
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(ggsignif)
library(readxl)
```

---

# 2. Check Working Directory

```r
getwd()
```

---

# 3. Set Working Directory

```r
setwd("G:/My Drive/Rhodes/2025/Honours 2025/Intro to R Hons 2025")
```

---

# 4. Import Dataset

```r
Independent_t_test <- read_excel("Independent_T_Test_Data.xlsx")

data2025 <- Independent_t_test

View(data2025)
```

---

# 5. Inspect the Data

```r
head(data2025)

str(data2025)
```

---

# 6. Perform Independent T-Test

```r
t_test_result <- t.test(`Plant richness` ~ Sites, data = data2025)

print(t_test_result)
```

---

# 7. Extract P-value

```r
p_value <- t_test_result$p.value

p_value
```

---

# 8. Plot Mean Values with Error Bars

```r
plot <- ggplot(data2025, aes(x = Sites, y = `Plant richness`, fill = Sites)) +

  geom_bar(stat = "summary",
           fun = "mean",
           position = "dodge",
           width = 0.6) +

  geom_errorbar(stat = "summary",
                fun.data = "mean_cl_boot",
                width = 0.25) +

  scale_fill_manual(values = c("red","blue")) +

  labs(title = "Species Richness by Site",
       x = "Sites",
       y = "Plant species richness") +

  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  theme_classic()

plot
```

---

# 9. Add Significance Annotation

```r
plot + geom_signif(comparisons = list(c("Invaded","Uninvaded")),
                   annotations = paste("p =", format(p_value, digits = 2)),
                   map_signif_level = TRUE) +

  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(face = "bold", color = "black", size = 12),
    axis.title.x = element_text(face = "bold", color = "black", size = 18),
    axis.line = element_line(color = "black"),

    axis.text.y = element_text(face = "bold", color = "black", size = 12),
    axis.title.y = element_text(face = "bold", color = "black", size = 18),

    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white"),

    legend.title = element_text(color = "black", size = 14),
    legend.text = element_text(color = "black", size = 14)
  )
```

---

# 10. Alternative Plot: Mean with Error Bars

```r
Errorplot <- ggplot(data2025, aes(x = Sites, y = `Plant richness`)) +

  geom_point(stat = "summary",
             fun = "mean",
             size = 3.8) +

  geom_errorbar(stat = "summary",
                fun.data = "mean_cl_boot",
                width = 0.25) +

  labs(x = "Sites",
       y = "Plant species richness") +

  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(face = "bold", color = "black", size = 12),
    axis.title.x = element_text(face = "bold", color = "black", size = 18),

    axis.line = element_line(color = "black"),

    axis.text.y = element_text(face = "bold", color = "black", size = 12),
    axis.title.y = element_text(face = "bold", color = "black", size = 18),

    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white"),

    legend.title = element_text(color = "black", size = 14),
    legend.text = element_text(color = "black", size = 14)
  )

Errorplot
```

---

# 11. Add Letter-Based Significance

```r
Errorplot +

  geom_text(
    aes(x = 1, y = 12, label = "a"),
    size = 6,
    fontface = "bold",
    color = "black"
  ) +

  geom_text(
    aes(x = 2, y = 17, label = "b"),
    size = 6,
    fontface = "bold",
    color = "black"
  )
```

---

# 12. Interpretation

If the **p-value < 0.05**, the two groups are significantly different.

If the **p-value > 0.05**, there is no significant difference between the means.

---

© 2026 Dr Lindokuhle Dlamini
